records.display_name: First Avenue/Second Avenue SBS

description: 8.5 miles from South Ferry to 125th Street. Currently served by M15 bus route. SBS corridor on First and Second Avenues in Manhattan.
SBS project along First and Second Avenues on the East Side of Manhattan, from South Ferry to 125th Street
Proposed SBS project on First and Second Avenues from South Ferry to 125th Street, replacing existing M15 Limited service
SBS along First Avenue and Second Avenue from South Ferry to 125th Street, launching October 10, 2010
M15 Select Bus Service along First Avenue and Second Avenue, adding over 10 miles of new and improved bus facilities and doubling protected bike lanes in New York City
Select Bus Service corridor on First and Second Avenues from 125th Street to South Ferry, currently served by M15 bus route
SBS service on First Avenue and Second Avenue from South Ferry to 125th Street, replacing M15 local bus route. Service begins October 2010.

Phase I BRT corridor identified by NYC BRT Study in 2006; 8.5 miles from South Ferry to 125th Street; includes bus lanes, transit signal priority, off-board fare collection, and protected bicycle paths
Phase I BRT corridor identified by NYC BRT Study in 2006, running 8.5 miles from South Ferry to 125th Street, currently served by M15 bus route

Implementation of Select Bus Service on First and Second Avenues from Houston Street to 125th Street, including bus lanes, off-board fare collection, low-floor three-door buses, transit signal priority, and bike lanes
Select Bus Service (SBS) on First and Second Avenues in Manhattan, launched M15 SBS October 10, 2010, with dedicated bus lanes, off-board fare payment, and revised station spacing.

Select Bus Service on First and Second Avenues in Manhattan, launched October 10, 2010 with dedicated bus lanes, NYPD and camera enforcement, off-board fare payment, revised station spacing, and low-floor three-door buses
Phase I BRT route planned to begin service in 2010 to help relieve subway crowding on Lexington Avenue lines
Phase I BRT route along First and Second Avenues in Manhattan; will help relieve subway crowding on Lexington Avenue lines
